commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Manual author or maintainer handling is needed because this draft protected PR has security, compatibility, and live-proof blockers before merge.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete sensitive-artifact risk by uploading complete live WhatsApp gateway heap snapshots.; Review finding: [P1] Keep heap snapshots out of uploaded live artifacts — .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml:734 - [P1] Preser... (sha=e69ca6b7025f394b6e9a4c2df2802ac823a51f7f)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes WhatsApp live QA, WhatsApp inbound monitoring, agent tool prep, provider/plugin hot paths, workflow env, tests, and changelog entries to reduce and trace WhatsApp live RTT.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blockers by source inspection: the head enables heap snapshots and copies them into uploaded artifacts, and the hydration predicate omits the current `allowFrom` fallback contract. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Manual author or maintainer handling is needed because this draft protected PR has security, compatibility, and live-proof blockers before merge.

target.title: fix(qa): reduce WhatsApp live RTT setup cost
target.body:
Summary
- Skip WhatsApp live QA agent workspace bootstrap and force exact-marker canaries to run with thinking disabled in both defaults and per-model params.
- Add per-scenario WhatsApp live timings, RSS samples, complete heap snapshot capture, and redacted gateway trace artifacts.
- Skip eager participating-group metadata hydration for DM-only WhatsApp sessions.
- Add changelog coverage for the QA and WhatsApp runtime changes.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krx90szx6z16pqh37w0206tf`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krx9ne1a4egr0qxpp22chkf5`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krxagz91ep6d1ca29qx5b00q`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0

Live evidence
- `26027068186` at `fdc2d0e9`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7532ms`; trace isolated ~`5141ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26027596921` at `74c4bb73`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7930ms`; trace isolated ~`5890ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26028278115` at `9ef7fbe1`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `10393ms`; trace isolated ~`7129ms` in agent/model before outbound send.

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp live QA canary artifacts now separate channel/provider cost from agent/model cost, capture complete heap snapshots, and avoid unnecessary setup/group hydration in the DM-only path.
Real environment tested: live WhatsApp Convex QA on GitHub Actions, plus Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gates.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa whatsapp --provider-mode live-frontier --model openai/gpt-5.5 --alt-model openai/gpt-5.5 --scenario whatsapp-canary` through `qa-live-transports-convex.yml`.
Evidence after fix: WhatsApp provider-path trace is now visible; the latest run shows inbound/provider/outbound overhead is small compared with agent/model time.
Observed result after fix: this branch improves observability and removes some setup work, but it does not yet reduce the live canary into the target range; current dominant cost is agent/model pre-outbound latency.
What was not tested: full workflow green is not claimed; the exact workflow runs may show unrelated lane failures after the WhatsApp job/artifact succeeded.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: fix(doctor): anchor WhatsApp TUI process matching
target.body:
## Summary
- replace broad WhatsApp doctor TUI process regex matching with executable/subcommand-aware matching
- only match `openclaw-tui` or `openclaw` with known local TUI subcommands
- add regression coverage for unrelated commands whose argv contains matching words

Fixes #83283.

## Real behavior proof

![PR 83313 before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83313-fresh/pr-83313/pr-83313-fix-doctor-anchor-whatsapp-tui-process-matching-before-after-proof.png)


Live WSL process-discovery proof added after ClawSweeper feedback:

```text
live ps rows used for proof:
 260353 openclaw-tui 45
 260354 openclaw tui
 260355 helper openclaw tui

listLocalTuiProcesses() result for proof pids:
[
  {
    "pid": 260353,
    "command": "openclaw-tui 45"
  },
  {
    "pid": 260354,
    "command": "openclaw tui"
  }
]
proof ok: real TUI processes detected; helper argv false positive ignored
```

Command: `bash .artifacts/pr-review/83313-live-ps-proof.sh` from the PR worktree under WSL Ubuntu. The helper process intentionally had `openclaw tui` in argv but was not returned because its executable was `helper`.
Fresh deterministic proof artifacts:
- Summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83313-fresh/pr-83313/summary.txt
- Before/source proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83313-fresh/pr-83313/before-source-proof.log
- After/PR proof log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83313-fresh/pr-83313/after-pr-83313.log

- Behavior or issue addressed: WhatsApp responsiveness repair should not select unrelated processes just because their argv contains words like `openclaw tui` or `openclaw terminal`.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-branches/fix-whatsapp-tui-process-match-83283`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts` and `pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
RUN  v4.1.6 /root/src/openclaw-branches/fix-whatsapp-tui-process-match-83283

✓ commands  ../../src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts (4 tests) 102ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)

[check:changed] src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.ts: core production
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

- Observed result after fix: mocked `ps` rows such as `helper --note 'openclaw tui'`, `openclaw-helper openclaw terminal`, and `openclaw --flag tui` are ignored; actual `openclaw-tui`, `openclaw tui --local`, and `/usr/bin/openclaw chat` remain recognized.
- What was not tested: sending signals to real local TUI processes.
- Before evidence (terminal/code capture from `origin/main`):

```text
src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.ts before this patch used a broad command-line regex:
  /(?:^|\s)(?:openclaw-tui|openclaw\s+tui|openclaw\s+chat|openclaw\s+terminal)(?:\s|$)/

That matched unrelated argv text such as:
  helper --note 'openclaw tui'
  openclaw-helper openclaw terminal
```

## Root Cause
- Root cause: process discovery treated the full command line as free text and matched any occurrence of OpenClaw TUI terms, instead of checking the executable and expected subcommand position.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression coverage for unrelated processes containing matching words in argv.
- Contributing context: the repair path passes discovered PIDs directly into the termination helper.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit test.
- Target test or file: `src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: process discovery accepts only expected TUI command shapes and rejects unrelated argv containing matching words.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to local process discovery before any signal is sent.

## Validation
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
target.author: giodl73-repo
